# Para revisar las opciones de compilacion y ejecucion,
# ingrese en el terminal el comando: make
#
#

PROB=subasta

ARCH=$(shell arch)

NTHSRC=nKernel/nthread.c nKernel/nutil.c nKernel/ntime.c nKernel/pss.c \
       nKernel/sched-fcfs.c nKernel/sched-rr.c \
       nKernel/sched-pri-1core.c nKernel/sched-fcfs-1core.c \
       nKernel/sem.c nKernel/mutex-cond.c nKernel/nmsgs.c \
       nKernel/nsystem.c

NTHHDR=nKernel/nthread.h nKernel/nthread-impl.h nKernel/pss.h

SRCS= test-$(PROB).c $(PROB).c $(NTHSRC)
HDRS= $(PROB).h $(NTHHDR)

#DEFS=-DNTHSTOP -DTRACESCHED
DEFS=

SHELL=bash -o pipefail

INCLUDE=-InKernel
CFLAGS=-Wall -Werror -pedantic -DNTHREADS -std=c18 $(INCLUDE) $(DEFS)
#CFLAGS=-Wall -Werror -pedantic -std=c18 $(INCLUDE)
LDLIBS=-lm -lpthread -lrt

MAK=make --no-print-directory

readme:
	@less README.txt

$(PROB).bin $(PROB).bin-g $(PROB).bin-san $(PROB).bin-thr: $(SRCS) $(HDRS)

run-san: $(PROB).bin-san
	@if grep -P '\t' $(PROB).c ; then echo "Su archivo $(PROB).c contiene tabs.  Reemplacelos por espacios en blanco!" ; false ; else true; fi
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin-san
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin-san -slice 1

run-thr: $(PROB).bin-thr
	@echo "No tiene sentido ejecutar sanitize para threads"
	@echo "porque no esta diseñado para nThread"
	@echo "No se extrañe por los errores que detecta"
	./$(PROB).bin-thr

run-g: $(PROB).bin-g
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin-g  
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin-g -slice 1 -ncores 4

run: $(PROB).bin
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin -ncores 4
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin -slice 1 -ncores 4

run-par: $(PROB).bin
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin -ncores 4
	@echo --------------------------------------------
	./$(PROB).bin -slice 1 -ncores 4

ddd: $(PROB).ddd

ddd-san: $(PROB).ddd-san

zip:
	@if grep -P '\t' $(PROB).c ; then echo "Su archivo $(PROB).c contiene tabs.  Reemplacelos por espacios en blanco!" ; false ; else true; fi
	@rm -f resultados.txt $(PROB).zip
	@echo "Sistema operativo utilizado" > resultados.txt
	@uname -a >> resultados.txt
	@cat resultados.txt
	@echo ==== run-san ==== | tee -a resultados.txt
	@$(MAK) -B run-san | tee -a resultados.txt
	@echo ==== run-g ==== | tee -a resultados.txt
	@$(MAK) -B run-g | tee -a resultados.txt
	@echo ==== run ==== | tee -a resultados.txt
	@$(MAK) -B run | tee -a resultados.txt
	@echo ==== zip ====
	zip -r $(PROB).zip resultados.txt $(PROB).c
	@echo "Archivo $(PROB).zip generado"
	@echo "Revise que contiene su ultima version de $(PROB).c"

%.bin: %.c
	gcc -g -O -DOPT=1 $(CFLAGS) $(SRCS) $(LDLIBS) -o $@

%.bin-g: %.c
	gcc -g $(CFLAGS) $(SRCS) $(LDLIBS) -o $@

%.bin-thr: %.c
	gcc -g -DSAN=1 -fsanitize=address $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDE) $(SRCS) $(LDLIBS) -o $@

%.bin-san: %.c
	gcc -g -DSAN=1 -fsanitize=address -fsanitize=undefined $(CFLAGS) $(SRCS) $(LDLIBS) -o $@

%.ddd: %.bin-g
	ddd $(*F).bin-g &

%.ddd-san: %.bin-san
	ddd $(*F).bin-san &

clean:
	rm -rf *.o *.log *.bin* core bin obj resultados.txt $(PROB).zip

Let me understand the task:

We need to implement a multi-threaded auction system using nThreads (a cooperative threading library). The key functions are:

1. `nNuevaSubasta(int n)` - Creates a new auction for n items. Called by the auction owner.
2. `nDestruirSubasta(nSubasta s)` - Frees resources.
3. `nAdjudicar(nSubasta s, int *punid)` - Called by owner to close the auction. Returns total amount collected and stores in *punid the number of unsold units (when fewer bidders than items).
4. `nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double oferta)` - Called by bidders. Returns TRUE when the bidder wins (gets a unit) and FALSE when they lose (higher bids already filled all items or timeout).

The task has two parts:
- T4: Implement with timeout = -1 (infinite wait)
- T5: Implement with timeout support

Looking at the test code, I notice:
- `nOfrecer` only takes `(nSubasta s, double precio)` — no timeout parameter in the header.
- But the assignment says there should be a timeout parameter. However, looking at the test file, `nOfrecer` is called as `nOfrecer(s, oferta)` with just two arguments.
- The header file also shows `int nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double precio);`

Wait, re-reading the assignment more carefully:

"La función nOfrecer queda a la espera hasta que:
i) La subasta se cierra, retornando TRUE cuando se adjudica un producto y FALSE cuando se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada."

So when the auction closes (nAdjudicar is called), all waiting bidders get a result:
- TRUE if they get a unit (their bid is among the top n bids)
- FALSE if they don't (there were n or more bidders with higher or equal prices)

Wait, let me re-read: "FALSE cuando se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada." So if there are already n bidders who offered more, the current bidder loses (FALSE).

And if timeout occurs, it returns FALSE.

But wait, there's also this: "ii) Si se produce el timeout el nthread retorna FALSE."

OK so the nOfrecer function signature in the header doesn't have a timeout parameter. But the task description mentions timeout = -1 for T4 and a timeout for T5.

Looking at the test file more carefully, I see it only uses `nOfrecer(s, oferta)` with 2 parameters. So maybe the timeout is handled internally? Or maybe the function signature was supposed to be different?

Wait, looking at the header again:
```c
int nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double precio);
```

And in the task description:
```c
int nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double oferta, int timeout);
```

There's a discrepancy. The header file has 2 parameters but the task description says 3 parameters. Let me check if the test file uses 2 or 3 parameters...

In test-subasta.c:
```c
int ret= nOfrecer(s, oferta);
```

It uses 2 parameters. So I should implement the function as declared in the header with 2 parameters (no timeout).

But the task says T5 should implement timeout. Maybe the task was for a different version? Let me look at what's expected more carefully.

Actually, looking at the Makefile, the tests are compiled with just test-subasta.c and subasta.c. The test file uses `nOfrecer(s, oferta)` with 2 params. So I should implement the header as given.

For T4, timeout = -1 means infinite wait. For T5, there's a timeout and the nthread can make a new offer.

But since the header doesn't have a timeout parameter, maybe I need to implement the basic version first (T4) that works with the existing tests.

content.text: Let me read the key files to understand the nThread API.

Now I understand the nThread API. Key points:

1. The tasks run under nThreads, which is a cooperative threading system with its own synchronization primitives.
2. I need to use `START_CRITICAL`, `END_CRITICAL`, `setReady`, `suspend`, `schedule` etc.
3. I cannot use pre-existing synchronization tools like semaphores, mutexes, conditions, or messages.

I can see in nthread-impl.h there are already states defined for "WAIT_SUBASTA" and "WAIT_SUBASTA_TIMEOUT" which are specifically for this task (Tareas 4 y 5 Sem 25/1).

Let me look at some of the example synchronization implementations to understand the patterns.

Let me look at sem.c for a simple example.

Now I have a good understanding of the nThread API and how to implement synchronization primitives.

Let me understand the auction system:

1. `nNuevaSubasta(int n)` - Creates an auction for n items. The owner creates this.

2. `nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double oferta)` - Called by bidders. A bidder offers a price for one item. The function blocks until:
   - The auction closes (nAdjudicar is called), and the bidder is among the top n bidders → returns TRUE (wins)
   - The auction closes and there are n or more better offers → returns FALSE (loses)
   - Timeout occurs → returns FALSE (T5)

Wait, re-reading the description more carefully:

"La función nOfrecer queda a la espera hasta que:
i) La subasta se cierra, retornando TRUE cuando se adjudica un producto y FALSE cuando se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada."

So during the auction, bidders arrive and offer prices. If at any point there are already n bidders with higher prices, the current bidder loses immediately (FALSE). If the auction closes and the bidder is among the top n, they win (TRUE).

Wait, let me re-read: "FALSE cuando se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada."

Hmm, this says: FALSE when n bidders with a price higher than the bidder's offer have presented themselves. So if there are n other bidders who bid higher, this bidder loses.

But actually, let me look at the test cases to understand the expected behavior better.

Test 1:
- Auction for 2 items
- pedro offers 1, juan offers 3, diego offers 4, pepe offers 2
- Expected: pedro loses (1 is low), pepe loses (2 is low)
- Then adjudicar with recaud=7 (from juan(3) + diego(4)), u=0
- juan wins, diego wins

So the top 2 bids win (3 and 4), the bottom 2 lose (1 and 2).

But the description also says: "FALSE cuando se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada."

So if there are already n bidders with higher offers... Let me think about this differently.

Actually, I think the behavior is:
- Multiple bidders call nOfrecer and block waiting.
- The auction runs until nAdjudicar is called.
- When nAdjudicar is called, it selects the top n bidders (highest offers) as winners.
- The winners get TRUE returned from nOfrecer.
- The losers get FALSE returned from nOfrecer.
- But there's also an optimization: if at any point, there are already at least n bidders with offers higher than your offer, you lose immediately (before the auction closes).

Wait, let me look at test 2 more carefully:

Test 2:
- Auction for 3 items
- ana offers 7, sleep 1s
- maria offers 3, sleep 1s
- ximena offers 4, sleep 1s
- erika offers 5, sleep 1s
- Expected: maria(3) loses (FALSE) - because there are already 3 higher offers? ana(7), ximena(4) hasn't been created yet, wait...

Actually, let me trace through:
1. ana offers 7 (first bidder, no comparison yet, blocks)
2. sleep 1s
3. maria offers 3 (second bidder, blocks)
4. sleep 1s
5. ximena offers 4 (third bidder, blocks)
6. sleep 1s
7. erika offers 5 (fourth bidder. Now we have 4 bidders for 3 items. erika's 5 is higher than maria's 3, so maria should lose? But maria was already waiting...)

Wait, the test says: after erika offers 5, they check `if (esperar(maria))` - maria should lose. So maria (3) loses because erika (5) offered more.

Then sonia offers 6. And `if (esperar(ximena))` - ximena (4) should lose. Now we have ana(7), erika(5), sonia(6) as top 3.

Then adjudicar returns 18 (7+5+6), u=0.

So the behavior is:
- As bidders arrive, if there are already n bidders with offers higher than this one, this bidder loses immediately.
- More precisely, at any point, the system keeps track of the top n bids seen so far.
- A new bidder whose offer is NOT in the top n (i.e., there are already n offers strictly greater than theirs) loses immediately with FALSE.
- When the auction closes (nAdjudicar), the current top n bidders win (TRUE), and any remaining waiting bidders lose (FALSE).

Wait, but what about bidders who arrive and their offer IS in the top n at the time they arrive? They wait (block) until the auction closes, at which point if they're still among the final top n, they win; otherwise they lose.

Actually, let me reconsider. Let me think about what "se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada" means.

It means: if there are n bidders who already presented a price higher than your offer, then you lose (FALSE).

So:
- The system maintains a collection of the best offers seen so far.
- A new offer is compared: if there are already n offers higher than it, the new offer loses immediately.
- Otherwise, the offer is recorded and the bidder waits.
- When the auction closes, the top n (or fewer if fewer bidders) win.

Let me re-examine test 2:
1. ana(7): No other offers, so she's in the top 3. Blocks.
2. maria(3): There are 0 offers > 3 so far (ana has 7 > 3, but that's only 1, not 3). So she blocks.
3. ximena(4): There is 1 offer > 4 (ana 7), not 3 yet. Blocks.
4. erika(5): There is 1 offer > 5 (ana 7), not 3 yet. But wait, erika should cause maria to lose...

Hmm, maybe the logic is different. Let me re-read:

"FALSE cuando se presentaron n oferentes con un precio mayor a la oferta realizada."

So if there are n bidders with higher offers, this bidder loses. But when erika(5) arrives, maria(3) had already arrived earlier. At that point, we have the following offers: ana(7), maria(3), ximena(4), erika(5). For maria(3), there are 3 bidders with higher prices (ana=7, ximena=4, erika=5). So maria loses.

But wait, ximena had 4 > 3 and erika had 5 > 3. But also, when maria first arrived, there was only ana(7) > 3 (1 bidder). So maria waited. Later, when enough higher bidders arrived (total 3 higher), maria lost.

So the logic is: a bidder blocks/wakes up when enough bidders with higher offers arrive, or when the auction closes.

Let me re-think the data structures:

We need:
- A queue/list of bidders who are currently waiting, sorted by offer price.
- When a new bidder arrives:
  - Their offer is compared with existing offers.
  - If there are already n offers >= theirs (or strictly >?), they lose immediately.
  - Otherwise, they're added to the waiting list and block.
  - After adding, check if any existing waiters now have n or more offers higher than theirs - those should lose.

- When the auction closes (nAdjudicar):
  - The top n (or all if fewer than n) waiting bidders win (TRUE).
  - The rest lose (FALSE).

Let me check with test 1:
- Auction for 2 items.
- pedro(1), juan(3), diego(4), pepe(2) all arrive "at the same time" (with random delays).
- The expected result: pedro(1) and pepe(2) lose; juan(3) and diego(4) win.
- Total recaudation: 7 (3+4), u=0.

So with 4 bidders for 2 items, the top 2 win and the bottom 2 lose. But when do they lose? They must lose before nAdjudicar is called because the test checks:
```c
if (esperar(pedro))
    fatalError("test1", "pedro debio perder con 1\n");
if (esperar(pepe))
    fatalError("test1", "pepe debio perder con 2\n");
```
These are checked BEFORE nAdjudicar. So pedro and pepe must have already returned FALSE before the auction closed.

And after nAdjudicar:
```c
if (!esperar(juan))
    fatalError("nMain", "juan debio ganar con 3\n");
if (!esperar(diego))
    fatalError("nMain", "diego debio ganar con 4\n");
```

```c
    s->n = n;
    s->cerrada = 0;
    s->lista = NULL;
    s->count = 0;
    return s;

void nDestruirSubasta(nSubasta s) {

// Insert offer in sorted position (descending)
static void insertarOferta(Oferta **lista, Oferta *of) {
    if (*lista == NULL || (*lista)->precio < of->precio) {
        of->sig = *lista;
        *lista = of;
    } else {
        Oferta *curr = *lista;
        while (curr->sig != NULL && curr->sig->precio >= of->precio) {
            curr = curr->sig;
        }
        of->sig = curr->sig;
        curr->sig = of;
    }
}

// Remove and return the last offer (lowest price)
static Oferta *quitarMenor(Oferta **lista) {
    if (*lista == NULL) return NULL;
    if ((*lista)->sig == NULL) {
        Oferta *of = *lista;
        *lista = NULL;
        return of;
    }
    Oferta *curr = *lista;
    while (curr->sig->sig != NULL) {
        curr = curr->sig;
    }
    Oferta *of = curr->sig;
    curr->sig = NULL;
    return of;
}

// Remove a specific offer
static void quitarOferta(Oferta **lista, Oferta *of) {
    if (*lista == of) {
        *lista = of->sig;
        return;
    }
    Oferta *curr = *lista;
    while (curr != NULL && curr->sig != of) {
        curr = curr->sig;
    }
    if (curr != NULL) {
        curr->sig = of->sig;
    }
}
```

```c
int nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double oferta) {
    START_CRITICAL
    
    if (s->cerrada) {
        END_CRITICAL
        return FALSE;
    }
    
    // Create offer entry
    Oferta *of = (Oferta *) nMalloc(sizeof(Oferta));
    of->th = nSelf();
    of->precio = oferta;
    of->resultado = -1;
    of->sig = NULL;
    
    // Insert in sorted list
    insertarOferta(&s->lista, of);
    s->count++;
    
    // If we have more than n offers, kick out the lowest
    if (s->count > s->n) {
        Oferta *perdedor = quitarMenor(&s->lista);
        s->count--;
        perdedor->resultado = FALSE;
        
        if (perdedor == of) {
            // This thread loses immediately
            nFree(of);
            END_CRITICAL
            return FALSE;
        } else {
            // Another thread loses
            setReady(perdedor->th);
            // Wake up the scheduler
            schedule();
        }
    }
    
    // If still here, this thread must wait
    if (of->resultado == -1) {
        suspend(WAIT_SUBASTA);
        schedule();
    }
    
    int res = of->resultado;
    quitarOferta(&s->lista, of);
    nFree(of);
    
    END_CRITICAL
    return res;
}
```

```c
    END_CRITICAL
    return total;
}
```

Actually, I should also check if there are any edge cases I might have missed. Let me think about potential issues:

1. **What if `nNuevaSubasta(0)` is called?** With n=0, every bidder would cause count > 0, so every bidder would immediately lose (FALSE). nAdjudicar would return 0 and *punid = 0. This seems reasonable.

2. **What if `nAdjudicar` is called twice?** The second call would find cerrada=1 and still proceed. But it would try to set ready on already-resolved threads. The threads might have already finished and been cleaned up. However, in the test, nAdjudicar is only called once. I could add a check but it's not needed for the tests.

3. **What if `nOfrecer` is called after `nAdjudicar`?** I check `s->cerrada` and return FALSE immediately. Good.

4. **Thread safety with multiple cores:** The START_CRITICAL/END_CRITICAL provide mutual exclusion across cores via the scheduler mutex. My linked list operations are all protected.

5. **Memory leaks:** Each offer is freed in nOfrecer after resolution. The remaining offers (if any) are freed in nDestruirSubasta. The subasta struct itself is freed in nDestruirSubasta. No leaks.

6. **Double-free safety:** `quitarOferta` is safe if the offer is not in the list (it just traverses to the end). And we only free once.

7. **The `punid` parameter in nAdjudicar:** In test-subasta.c, it's always passed (&u), so it's not NULL. But I handle NULL anyway.

8. **The `resultado` field:** Value -1 means pending, 0 means FALSE (lose), 1 means TRUE (win). This is consistent.

content.text: Everything looks good. Let me provide a summary of the solution.

La solución está completa. Aquí un resumen:

## Implementación de Sistema de Subastas Multi-threaded

### Archivo modificado: `subasta.c`

**Estructuras:**

- `Oferta` — Cada oferta contiene: el thread oferente, el precio ofrecido, el resultado (pendiente/TRUE/FALSE), y un puntero al siguiente en la lista enlazada.
- `struct subasta` — Contiene: número de ítems, contador de oferentes activos, flag de cierre, y lista enlazada ordenada de ofertas.

**Algoritmo:**

1. **`nNuevaSubasta(int n)`** — Crea la subasta con `n` ítems, inicializa lista vacía.

2. **`nOfrecer(nSubasta s, double oferta)`** — Dentro de sección crítica:
   - Si la subasta ya cerró, retorna FALSE inmediatamente.
   - Crea una entrada de oferta y la inserta en la lista ordenada por precio descendente.
   - Si hay más oferentes que ítems (`count > n`), se elimina el de menor precio (el último de la lista) y se le retorna FALSE. Si el eliminado es el propio thread, retorna FALSE inmediatamente; si es otro, se despierta con `setReady`.
   - Si el thread sigue en la subasta (resultado pendiente), se suspende con `WAIT_SUBASTA` hasta que:
     - La subasta cierre (`nAdjudicar`) y el thread resulte ganador (TRUE) o perdedor (FALSE).
     - Sea expulsado por una oferta mayor (FALSE).
   - Al despertar, retorna el resultado.

3. **`nAdjudicar(nSubasta s, int *punid)`** — Dentro de sección crítica:
   - Marca la subasta como cerrada.
   - Los primeros `min(n, count)` oferentes (los de mayor precio) son ganadores (TRUE).
   - El resto (si los hay) son perdedores (FALSE).
   - Calcula recaudación total y unidades no vendidas (`*punid`).
   - Llama `schedule()` para que los threads despertados puedan ejecutar y luego retorna.

4. **`nDestruirSubasta(nSubasta s)`** — Libera cualquier oferta remanente y la subasta.

### Pruebas ejecutadas exitosamente:
- `make run` ✓ (FCFS, RR con 1 y 4 cores)
- `make run-g` ✓ (Debug, RR con 4 cores)
- `make run-thr` ✓ (AddressSanitizer)
- `make run-san` ✓ (AddressSanitizer + UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer)

### Instrucciones:
```bash
cd /workspace
make clean        # Limpiar binarios
make run          # Compilar y ejecutar todos los tests
make run-g        # Modo debug
make run-san      # Con sanitizers
make run-thr      # Con address sanitizer
make zip          # Generar subasta.zip con resultados
```

### Supuestos adoptados:
- La función `nOfrecer` sigue la firma del archivo de cabecera (2 parámetros, sin timeout), correspondiente a T4.
- Cuando hay empate en precios, el criterio de desempate es FIFO (el que llegó primero permanece).
- La sección crítica (`START_CRITICAL`/`END_CRITICAL`) provee exclusión mutua incluso en multi-core, reutilizando el mutex del planificador de nThreads.
